message.content: Speculative multi-token prediction boosts local decoding throughput by letting a cheaper draft model propose several candidate tokens ahead of time, which the full model then verifies in a single forward pass; since early transformer layers often
message.reasoning_content: Speculative multi-token prediction improves local decoding throughput by allowing the model to evaluate multiple candidate tokens in parallel during a single forward pass, rather than decoding one token at a time; this leverages the fact that early transformer layers often produce reasonably accurate token predictions, enabling the model to "draft" several tokens ahead while the full model verifies them, effectively reducing the number of expensive full-model forward passes needed per output token. However, speculative decoding may not help when the draft model's predictions are poor relative to the target model—such as in domains with high linguistic complexity or when the draft model is significantly smaller or less aligned—because the verification cost of accepting few tokens and re-running the full model for rejected ones can outweigh the benefits of parallel evaluation.
